name: Dwight D. Eisenhower
description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)
abstract: Dwight David Eisenhower (born David Dwight Eisenhower; October 14, 1890 – March 28, 1969), also known as Ike, was the 34th president of the United States from 1953 to 1961. In his previously military career, he led the Allied Expeditionary Force during the Second World War, launching decisive campaigns in North Africa and Normandy and be…

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions President Eisenhower only in the context of a cancelled concert scheduled as part of his visit to Moscow, stating 'The concert, by the way, was finally scheduled as part of President Eisenhower’s visit to Moscow, which didn’t happen.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Wiggs
about_quotes.evidence: I have a record of Oscar Salastain's memorial record. You remember when he went to play for President Eisenhower back in the early 50s, I think it was?

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: a whole lot of people that you might know came from Rock Hill, like President Eisenhower. He used to be down there and he used to borrow some of my uncle's dogs to hunt with

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Papa you know when we went to Washington and played for a command performance with President Eisenhower.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: President Eisenhower sent us behind the Iron Curtain in 1958.
